other_observers: Bill Schmoker, Doug Faulkner, Joel Such, Marcel Such.; Larry Semo and Steve Mlodinow
specific_location: Golden Ponds Park on the west side of Longmont.
description: This was a medium sized duck, very similar in size to the adjacent American Wigeons. It had an all dark head, with a medium sized tuft coming from the top of the back of the head. The eye was bright yellow. The bill was mostly a blueish gray, with a black tip and nail. The back and tail were a pretty dark shade of black, but not jet black. The breast was dark, but the sides were gray with the shade differing whether it was in direct sunlight or not. When it stretched its wings, it had an extensive wing stripe (like a Greater Scaup) which was bright white and not gray. The legs were dark and did not have any bands.Behaviors: The bird was in a small patch of open water where it dived quite frequently, identifying it as a diving duck not a dabbling duck. It also stood on the ice some giving an excellent look at the leg color.Call: None heard.Plumage: 1st winter
